perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, the Sponsor will be a co-presenting sponsor of FLOODfest Chicago at Virgin Hotel, which entitles the Sponsor to the following: (i) Sponsor will be a non-exclusive co-presenting sponsor of the Event.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, the Sponsor will be a co-presenting sponsor of FLOODfest Chicago at Virgin Hotel, which entitles the Sponsor to the following: (i) Sponsor will be the exclusive co-presenting sponsor of the Event.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive' creates a contradiction. Section 4 states ANTHEMIC will not discuss sponsorships with other video app companies before April 1, 2016, implying exclusivity. However, the term 'co-presenting sponsor' along with this perturbation that makes Sponsor 'exclusive' creates an in-text contradiction with the rights granted to the Sponsor, that may not be the only sponsor for that particular event.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Before April 1, 2016 ANTHEMIC will not discuss in any manner with any person or entity (other than the Sponsor) to be a sponsor of the Event in the Video App category. If ANTHEMIC produces the Event in 2016 and seeks a sponsor in the category, ANTHEMIC will first contact the Sponsor and provide the Sponsor with written notice (the "Notice) of the terms under which the Sponsor can be the category sponsor for the 2016 Event. The Sponsor will have 15 days from receipt of the Notice to accept the terms to be the category sponsor of the 2014 Event. If the Sponsor decides not to be the category sponsor of the 2016 Event or fails to timely respond to the Notice, then ANTHEMIC may approach other parties to be the category sponsor.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Sponsor will be a 'non-exclusive' co-presenting sponsor. The changed text modifies this to 'exclusive' co-presenting sponsor. The 'Contradicted Text' states that ANTHEMIC will not discuss sponsorships with other video app companies before April 1, 2016, and that if they want to seek sponsorship in the category after that date, they will contact the Sponsor first. This implies an option for exclusive sponsorship but doesn't guarantee it for the entire term. Changing 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive' directly conflicts with the implicit non-exclusivity before April 1, 2016, and the potential for other sponsors after that date if the Sponsor declines, creating an in-text contradiction that impacts enforcement of the rights granted to the Sponsor, per the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: ANTHEMIC anticipates that the Event will be held regardless of the weather. If the Event cannot be held on the scheduled dates, ANTHEMIC will make good faith efforts to re-schedule the Event. The Sponsor must provide the consideration in Section 2 regardless of whether the Event is actually held.
perturbation.changed_text: ANTHEMIC anticipates that the Event will be held regardless of the weather. If the Event cannot be held on the scheduled dates, ANTHEMIC is not obligated to re-schedule the Event. The Sponsor is entitled to a full refund if the Event is not held.
perturbation.explanation: The original section stated that the event would be held regardless of the weather, and if not, ANTHEMIC would try to reschedule, and the sponsor still had to pay. The changed text now states that ANTHEMIC is not obligated to reschedule, and that Sponsor will be entitled to a full refund. This creates an internal contradiction within Section 8, which causes ambiguity on the payment and the holding of the event. This creates an in-text contradiction as the original text says the sponsor must provide the consideration in Section 2 regardless of whether the Event is actually held, which is not being the case.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Rain or Shine. ANTHEMIC anticipates that the Event will be held regardless of the weather. If the Event cannot be held on the scheduled dates, ANTHEMIC is not obligated to re-schedule the Event. The Sponsor is entitled to a full refund if the Event is not held.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction regarding the sponsor's payment obligation. The original text states the Sponsor must provide consideration regardless of whether the event is held. The changed text entitles the Sponsor to a full refund if the event is not held. These clauses directly conflict, creating an in-text contradiction as they create conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties for both parties (Sponsor and ANTHEMIC). This follows the definition of inconsistencies that lead to in-text contradictions.
